examples.title: CFG for Balanced Parentheses
examples.description: Grammar to generate strings of balanced parentheses like (), ()(), (()), etc.
examples.explanation: S is start symbol, produces either concatenation of two balanced strings, or parentheses around a balanced string, or empty. This generates language L = { w | w is balanced parentheses }

examples.title: CFG to PDA Conversion Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to convert CFG to equivalent Pushdown Automaton
examples.explanation: Any CFG can be converted to PDA. The PDA simulates leftmost derivations by pushing RHS of productions, then matching/popping terminals when read. This proves CFL ⊆ Languages accepted by PDA.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for construction, O(n²) worst-case parse

examples.title: CNF Conversion Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to convert any CFG to Chomsky Normal Form
examples.code:
Algorithm toCNF(G):
 // Step 1: Remove ε-productions
 Find nullable variables (derives ε)
 For each production A → α with nullable vars:
 Generate new productions without nullable symbols
 Remove original ε-productions (except S→ε if S nullable)
 
 // Step 2: Remove unit productions (A→B)
 Compute unit pairs using transitive closure
 For each unit pair (A,B) and production B→α:
 Add A→α (if not unit production)
 Remove all unit productions
 
 // Step 3: Remove useless symbols
 Remove symbols not deriving terminal strings
 Remove symbols not reachable from S
 
 // Step 4: Convert to binary form
 For each production A → X1X2...Xk where k > 2:
 Create new variables Y1...Y(k-2)
 Replace with A → X1Y1, Y1 → X2Y2, ..., Y(k-2) → X(k-1)Xk
 
 // Final: All productions are A→BC or A→a
examples.explanation: CNF conversion ensures every production is either A→BC (two non-terminals) or A→a (single terminal). This simplifies parsing algorithms and is required for CYK parsing algorithm.

examples.title: CYK Parsing Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to check if string w belongs to language generated by CFG in CNF
examples.explanation: CYK is a dynamic programming algorithm for parsing strings against CFG in CNF. Time complexity is O(n³ × |P|) where n is string length and |P| is number of productions.
